response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist software engineer with broad full-stack, low-latency, and some embedded/hardware exposure, but they miss the core requirements of this role: no native-level Japanese proficiency (English/Romanian/basic German only), no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and no background in network security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or switch/L2-L3 development. Their C experience (NI LabWindows/CVI components) is dated relative to their recent TypeScript/Node.js focus, making them a poor fit for an embedded LAN-switch security position.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency systems work in DLang with async I/O, caching, and high-throughput microservices
Strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, teaching/workshops)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement (native-level)
No Linux kernel analysis/modification, network switch, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms, far from embedded C/UNIX firmware development

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering depth and systems-level (D/C++) background
